ci: replace fixed sleeps with juju-wait to fix flaky test timing

- Auto-update charm upgrade steps: replace sleep 90 with juju-wait
  so upgrade-charm + config-changed (npm install) have enough time
- Browser disable test: replace sleep 30 with juju-wait so
  config-changed completes before checking openclaw.json

  handle transient machine 'lost' connection during long npm install

test past the infinite reinstall loop, revealing the upstream timing
issues. The browser and node-version-config failures are CI flakiness
from insufficient waits rather than charm logic bugs.

fix webgl

* fix grid

* chore(ai): add WebGL Copilot instruction file scoped to lib/webgl and WebGL scenes

.github/instructions/webgl.instructions.md — project-specific guide for
GitHub Copilot, scoped via applyTo to front/src/lib/webgl/** and
front/src/components/scenes/WebGL*.

Written from scratch (the auto-generated skill was discarded because it
used WebGL 1 GLSL 100 syntax, JavaScript instead of TypeScript, and
generic patterns that don't match this project).

Covers: WebGL2-only policy, GLSL 300 ES required syntax, scene module
pattern (init → render loop → cleanup), shader compilation helpers,
VAO/VBO geometry generation, mouse distortion pattern, React island
wrapper convention, performance rules, and common pitfalls.

* fix(three): make Spline scene background transparent to show WebGL grid

Change setClearColor from opaque purple (0x0d1030, 1) to fully transparent
(0x000000, 0). The renderer already has alpha: true, so the canvas now
composites over the white distortion grid behind it.

* fix(webgl): make grid perfectly uniform with equal-size squares

- Remove power-curve spacing from generateGridVertices — lines are now
  uniformly distributed (t * extent instead of sign(t) * |t|^1.3 * extent)
- Remove stretch warp from vertex shader (was bending lines outward)
- Remove breathing animation from vertex shader (was pulsing line positions)
- Only the mouse distortion remains — lines are perfectly straight at rest

- Extract shaderUtils.ts (compile/link) and gridMesh.ts (geometry)

* feat(maintenance): floating CTA with collision detection and responsive sizing

- Add floatingCta.ts: bouncing CTA that reflects off viewport edges,

commit.message:
feat: add SSRF protection to FetchUrlTask with dynamic entitlements (#405)

* fix(tasks): replace regex SSRF check with entitlement-gated SafeFetch

The previous `isPrivateUrl` in FetchUrlTask matched hostnames against a
regex list, leaving trivial bypass paths: DNS rebinding (public name →
private IP between check and connect), IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:127.0.0.1`),
decimal/hex/octal IPv4 literals (`2130706433`, `0x7f000001`), and
trailing-dot metadata hosts. A global `WORKGLOW_ALLOW_PRIVATE_URLS` /
`VITE_WORKGLOW_ALLOW_PRIVATE_URLS` env kill-switch shipped the SSRF
defense disabled in any build with the flag on.

This change wires private-network access through the existing entitlements
system. `FetchUrlTask` now declares `network:private` dynamically (scoped
to the URL origin) whenever its URL is classified as private; the graph
runner denies the run unless the enforcer grants it. The env kill-switch
is removed entirely.

New files:
- `packages/tasks/src/util/UrlClassifier.ts` — pure, browser-safe
  classifier backed by `ipaddr.js`. Parses IPv4 in all inet_aton forms
  (decimal, hex, octal, 1/2/3/4-part), normalizes IPv6 brackets and
  trailing dots, detects IPv4-mapped IPv6, matches metadata/mDNS/internal
  suffixes, and rejects non-http(s) and credential URLs.
- `packages/tasks/src/util/SafeFetch.ts` — DI boundary. Default browser
  impl classifies + delegates to `globalThis.fetch`.
- `packages/tasks/src/util/SafeFetch.server.ts` — Node/Bun impl. Pre-resolves
  every A/AAAA record via `node:dns/promises`, rejects if any is private
  (rebinding defense), and pins the TCP connection to the validated IP via
  an undici `Agent` with a `connect.lookup` hook, closing the TOCTOU gap.
  Installed at `packages/tasks/src/{node,bun}.ts` via `registerSafeFetch`.

Entitlement profile:
- `BROWSER_GRANTS` narrowed from broad `network` to explicit `network:http`
  + `network:websocket`. The broad grant implicitly covered `network:private`
  via hierarchy — a leak that defeated SSRF enforcement.

Tests (169 pass across 4 suites):
- New `packages/test/src/test/task/FetchUrlSsrf.test.ts`: 35 tests covering
  classifier edge cases (all legacy IPv4 forms, IPv4-mapped IPv6, trailing
  dots, suffix patterns, credentials, invalid schemes), SafeFetch gating,
  dynamic entitlement declaration, and end-to-end graph enforcement (browser
  profile denies; dev-augmented profile with scoped loopback grants allows;
  LAN is not covered by a loopback grant; the removed env var has no effect).
- Existing `FetchTask.test.ts` and `FileLoaderTask.test.ts` updated to mock
  via `registerSafeFetch` instead of stubbing `global.fetch`.

New peer dep: `undici` (optional, for server-side connection pinning).
New dep: `ipaddr.js` (browser-safe IP parsing/classification).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FXuUR7Y3wKrwFTBe4soHMv

* fix(tasks): apply PR review feedback for SSRF protection improvements

- registerSafeFetch now returns previous impl; add getSafeFetchImpl() and resetSafeFetch() helpers
- Add redirect-following SSRF protection with manual redirect handling and per-hop re-validation (both browser and server impls)
- Fix agent/socket leak in server implementation using TransformStream pipeTo cleanup
- Move undici from devDependencies/optionalDependencies to regular dependencies
- entitlements() fails closed when URL is unknown (requires network:private by default)
- Tests use registerSafeFetch's returned value to save/restore previous SafeFetch impl
- Update test expecting fail-closed entitlements() behavior when URL is missing

commit.message:
feat(artifacts): Per-session artifact store with quota enforcement (#116)

* feat(artifacts): per-session artifact store with quota enforcement (#35)

- Add ToolContext interface (required on Tool.execute) for dependency injection
- Add ArtifactStoreQuota with configurable maxArtifacts (100) and maxSizeBytes (10MB)
- Add ArtifactQuotaExceededError thrown at write time when limits exceeded
- InMemoryArtifactStore now tracks total content size for O(1) enforcement
- Tools use context.artifactStore — no singleton fallback (strict isolation)
- MCP server creates per-session InMemoryArtifactStore in handleKickstart
- generate-manifests handler writes generated files to session.artifactStore
- Update all 11 core tools to accept ToolContext parameter
- Add quota enforcement, session isolation, and context injection tests
- All 506 tests pass, zero new lint errors

Addresses reviewer feedback:
- Zapp: strict session isolation (no singleton fallback), memory quota enforcement
- Leela: ToolContext required (not optional), eliminates fallback branches

Closes #35

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: append Bender history for #35

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: close 4 security gaps in artifact store (PR #116 review)

1. Remove singleton fallback in web ArtifactProvider — store prop is now
   required; no defaultArtifactStore import in the web layer. main.tsx
   creates and injects a session-scoped InMemoryArtifactStore explicitly.

2. Count metadata size in quota — artifactSizeBytes() now includes
   JSON.stringify(metadata).length alongside content bytes. Quota
   enforcement, delete, and replace all account for metadata.

3. Pass ToolContext in converse.ts — both non-streaming and streaming
   tool execution paths now create an InMemoryArtifactStore per request
   and pass it as ToolContext. No more bare tool.execute(args) calls.

4. Sanitize artifact content on storage — sanitizeContent() strips
   <script>, <style>, <iframe>, <object>, <embed> tags and on*= event
   handlers from non-HTML artifacts. HTML/XML/SVG artifacts are exempt.

Tests: 43 artifact-store tests pass (12 new), 518 total tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
